cpt_description: Electrocardiogram (ECG) with interpretation
billing_rule: ECG is not routinely covered for uncomplicated hypertension. Coverage requires documented symptoms such as chest pain, palpitations, or shortness of breath. Routine screening ECG without symptoms is denied under most Medicare and commercial payers.
frequency_limit: Once per year for symptomatic patients

cpt_description: Hemoglobin A1c (HbA1c) test
billing_rule: HbA1c testing covered up to 4 times per year for Type 2 diabetics. More than 4 tests per year require documentation of poor glycemic control or medication changes. Billing beyond this frequency without justification causes automatic denial.
frequency_limit: 4x per year standard; additional tests need documentation

cpt_description: Chest X-Ray, single view
billing_rule: Chest X-ray for acute bronchitis requires clinical suspicion of pneumonia — documented lung crackles, fever above 38.5C, or pulse oximetry below 95%. Routine chest X-ray for uncomplicated bronchitis is denied. Documentation must explicitly mention clinical indicators.

cpt_description: CT scan of abdomen without contrast
billing_rule: CT abdomen for unspecified abdominal pain requires documented failure of conservative treatment (minimum 4-6 weeks of conservative management, dietary changes, or trial medications). Pre-authorization required by most payers. Clinical notes must rule out simpler diagnoses before imaging approval.
frequency_limit: Once per acute episode with prior auth

cpt_description: Physical Therapy Evaluation, low complexity
billing_rule: First 10 physical therapy visits are automatically covered by most payers for low back pain when supported by a physician referral and active treatment plan. Additional visits beyond 10 require documented functional improvement and updated treatment plan. Medicare requires therapy to be deemed 'medically necessary and skilled'.

cpt_description: Echocardiography with Doppler
billing_rule: Echocardiogram is covered for heart failure patients to assess ejection fraction, valve function, and hemodynamics. Initial echo at diagnosis is covered without prior auth. Follow-up echos require documented change in clinical status or medication adjustment. Routine annual echo without clinical change is denied.
frequency_limit: Once at diagnosis; follow-ups need clinical justification

cpt_description: MRI Brain with and without contrast
billing_rule: Brain MRI for new-onset or changing migraines is covered with documented neurological symptoms (visual aura, focal deficits, first/worst headache). Routine MRI for stable, previously evaluated migraine is denied. Pre-authorization required by most commercial payers. Documentation must include symptom timeline and prior treatment history.
frequency_limit: Once per new presentation; not routinely annually

icd_description: Major Depressive Disorder, single episode, moderate
cpt_description: Psychotherapy, 60 minutes
billing_rule: Mental health parity laws require coverage of psychotherapy equal to medical services. Up to 20 sessions per year typically covered without prior auth. Beyond 20 sessions requires documented treatment plan, measurable goals, and evidence of therapeutic progress. Cannot bill 90837 (60 min) and 90834 (45 min) on same date.

icd_description: Encounter for general adult medical examination without abnormal findings
cpt_description: Preventive visit, established patient 18-39 years
billing_rule: Annual wellness exam covered once per calendar year. Billing a problem-focused E&M code on the same day as a preventive visit requires a 25 modifier unless entirely separate and distinct service. Copay waived under ACA for preventive services. Common denial: billed twice within 12 months.

cpt_description: Total Knee Arthroplasty (Knee Replacement)
billing_rule: Knee replacement surgery requires documented failure of conservative treatment for minimum 3-6 months including weight management, physical therapy, NSAIDs, and possibly corticosteroid injections. Pre-authorization required. Radiographic evidence of severe joint space narrowing (Grade 3-4 OA on Kellgren-Lawrence scale) must be documented.

cpt_description: Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH) assay
billing_rule: TSH testing covered for diagnosis and monitoring of hypothyroidism. Monitoring frequency: every 6-12 months once stable on medication. More frequent testing covered when adjusting levothyroxine dose or symptomatic change. Billing TSH with T3/T4 (84480, 84481) on same date requires clinical justification for additional tests.
frequency_limit: Every 6-12 months when stable; more frequent during dose adjustment

icd_description: Atherosclerotic heart disease of native coronary artery without angina
cpt_description: Treadmill Stress Test (Exercise ECG)
billing_rule: Stress testing for coronary artery disease requires documented symptoms (chest pain, exertional dyspnea) or risk stratification post-MI. Routine stress test for asymptomatic stable CAD is denied. Cannot bill stress test within 90 days of cardiac catheterization without documented new symptoms. Pre-auth often required by commercial payers.
frequency_limit: Once per symptomatic episode; annually if high-risk

icd_description: Encounter for screening for malignant neoplasm of colon
cpt_description: Diagnostic Colonoscopy
billing_rule: Screening colonoscopy covered every 10 years for average-risk adults starting at age 45 under most Medicare and commercial plans (2021 ACS guideline). If polyps removed, next colonoscopy interval based on pathology — typically 3-5 years. High-risk patients (family history of CRC) covered every 5 years. Billing diagnostic colonoscopy as screening or vice versa causes denial.
frequency_limit: Every 10 years (average risk); 5 years (high risk); 3-5 years post-polypectomy

icd_description: Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD) with acute exacerbation
cpt_description: Spirometry (Pulmonary Function Test)
billing_rule: Spirometry is required for COPD diagnosis confirmation and staging. Post-bronchodilator FEV1/FVC < 0.70 confirms COPD. Annual monitoring covered. Billing spirometry during acute exacerbation visit is denied — testing should be deferred until patient is stable. Cannot bill spirometry same day as bronchodilator challenge test without modifier.
frequency_limit: Annually for monitoring; not during acute exacerbation

icd_description: Malignant neoplasm of unspecified site of right female breast
cpt_description: Bilateral Screening Mammography
billing_rule: Annual screening mammography covered for women 40+ under most plans (50+ under some Medicare plans). Diagnostic mammography (after abnormal screening) billed differently and may have cost-sharing. 3D mammography (tomosynthesis) covered by most commercial payers but may require prior auth for Medicare in some jurisdictions. Cannot bill screening and diagnostic mammography same day.

icd_description: Epilepsy, unspecified, not intractable, without status epilepticus
cpt_description: Electroencephalogram (EEG), awake and drowsy
billing_rule: EEG covered for new seizure evaluation and epilepsy monitoring. Initial EEG after first seizure is covered without prior auth. Routine surveillance EEG for stable, well-controlled epilepsy requires clinical justification (breakthrough seizures, medication change). Cannot bill extended EEG monitoring (95812) and routine EEG (95816) same day.
frequency_limit: At diagnosis and when clinically indicated; not routine annual

cpt_description: Arthrocentesis (joint injection) of major joint
billing_rule: Corticosteroid joint injections covered for active inflammation not controlled by DMARDs. Maximum of 4 injections per joint per year recommended. Cannot bill bilateral joint injections with a single units code — must use modifier 50. Documentation must include joint swelling, warmth, and range-of-motion limitations. Pre-auth required for biologic therapy administration via injection.
frequency_limit: Maximum 4 injections per joint per year

icd_description: Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia (BPH) without lower urinary tract symptoms
cpt_description: Transurethral resection of prostate (TURP)
billing_rule: TURP for BPH requires documented failure of medical management (alpha-blockers, 5-alpha reductase inhibitors) for minimum 6 months, with documented AUA symptom score >7. Pre-authorization required. Urodynamic studies (51736-51785) may be required before surgical approval particularly when diagnosis is uncertain.

cpt_description: Laser treatment for psoriasis, <250 sq cm
billing_rule: Laser treatment (excimer or PUVA) for psoriasis requires documented failure of topical treatments (steroids, vitamin D analogues) and phototherapy. Most payers require step therapy — topicals first, then conventional phototherapy (96900), then laser. Pre-auth required. BSA involvement and PASI score must be documented.
frequency_limit: Typically 2-3x per week, up to 30 sessions per authorization

cpt_description: Cataract surgery with insertion of intraocular lens (IOL)
billing_rule: Cataract surgery covered when visual acuity is 20/50 or worse in the operative eye OR when cataract interferes with daily activities despite glasses. Best corrected visual acuity must be documented. Premium IOL (toric, multifocal) implants are NOT covered by Medicare — patient pays the difference. Cannot bill bilateral cataract surgery on same date of service.

icd_description: Encounter for supervision of normal first pregnancy, unspecified trimester
cpt_description: Ultrasound, pregnant uterus, first trimester
billing_rule: One ultrasound per trimester is standard coverage for low-risk pregnancies. First trimester ultrasound (76801) covered for dating, viability confirmation, and nuchal translucency screening. Additional ultrasounds require clinical indication (bleeding, pain, abnormal findings). Cannot bill 76801 and 76813 (NT scan) on same date without documentation of distinct clinical purpose.
frequency_limit: One per trimester (standard risk); additional with clinical indication

cpt_description: HIV-1 RNA quantitative PCR (Viral Load)
billing_rule: HIV viral load testing covered every 3-4 months for patients on antiretroviral therapy (ART). Testing every 6 months covered for virologically stable patients with viral load <200 copies/mL for >2 years. Additional tests covered when changing ART regimen or clinical deterioration. CD4 count (86360) billed separately — cannot use same CPT for both.
frequency_limit: Every 3-4 months on ART; every 6 months if stable

icd_description: Fracture of navicular bone of right wrist, initial encounter
cpt_description: X-Ray of wrist, 2 views
billing_rule: Wrist X-ray is covered for suspected fracture after trauma. Initial fracture X-ray does not require prior auth. Follow-up X-rays for fracture healing are covered at 6 weeks and 3 months post-injury. MRI for suspected occult fracture not seen on X-ray is covered when X-ray is negative but clinical suspicion remains high (snuffbox tenderness). Documentation must include mechanism of injury.
frequency_limit: Initial + follow-ups at 6 weeks and 3 months

cpt_description: External Ambulatory Cardiac Monitor (Holter), up to 48 hours
billing_rule: Holter monitoring covered for evaluation of symptomatic arrhythmia (palpitations, syncope, presyncope). Initial Holter covered without prior auth. Extended monitoring (30-day event monitor, 93268) may require prior auth and is indicated when standard Holter fails to capture symptoms. Cannot bill Holter and in-office ECG (93000) on same date for same indication.

cpt_description: Polysomnography, full night, attended
billing_rule: In-lab polysomnography covered after clinical evaluation confirms moderate-to-high OSA risk (Epworth score >10, witnessed apneas, nocturnal hypoxemia symptoms). Home sleep apnea test (HSAT, 95800) is preferred first-line for uncomplicated cases by most payers. In-lab study required for complex cases (COPD, heart failure, neuromuscular disease). Documentation must include clinical risk assessment.
frequency_limit: Once for diagnosis; repeat if treatment fails or clinical change

cpt_description: Hemodialysis procedure
billing_rule: Hemodialysis covered under Medicare Part B for ESRD (stage 5) patients. Stage 3 CKD does NOT qualify for dialysis coverage — this combination will be denied. Dialysis is covered only when eGFR is consistently <15 mL/min/1.73m² (stage 5) or when uremia symptoms are present regardless of eGFR in acute settings. Common coding error: billing hemodialysis CPT with early CKD ICD code.

cpt_description: Emergency Department visit, high complexity
billing_rule: High-complexity ED visit (99285) requires documentation of high medical decision-making: multiple diagnoses considered, extensive data reviewed, and high risk of complications. For chest pain, must document ECG interpretation, troponin results, and risk stratification (HEART score or TIMI score). Billing 99285 without meeting all MDM criteria triggers downcoding to 99283/99284. Observation admission (G0378) cannot be billed same day as high-complexity ED visit.

icd_description: Malignant neoplasm of upper lobe, unspecified bronchus or lung
cpt_description: Chemotherapy administration, intravenous infusion, up to 1 hour
billing_rule: Chemotherapy administration requires prior authorization for all commercial payers. Treatment plan must be approved before first infusion. NCCN guideline-concordant regimens are preferred for approval. Off-label drug use requires additional documentation of clinical evidence. Cannot bill E&M code (99213-99215) and chemotherapy administration (96413) on same day without separate and distinct medical necessity documentation.
frequency_limit: Per treatment cycle per approved regimen

icd_description: Encounter for routine child health examination with abnormal findings
cpt_description: Preventive office visit, age 1-4 years
billing_rule: Well-child visits follow AAP periodicity schedule. Each visit covered once per applicable age range under most plans. Cannot bill sick-visit E&M and well-child visit on same day without modifier 25, and only if the sick visit was completely separate and distinct. Immunizations administered during well-child visit billing requires separate vaccine codes with administration CPT 90460. Downcoding risk if documentation does not match age-specific requirements.

cpt_description: Office outpatient visit, established patient, low-moderate complexity
billing_rule: Insulin management visits covered as standard E&M visits. Documentation must support the selected complexity level — 99213 (low-moderate) requires 2 of 3: 2+ chronic conditions addressed, independent interpretation of test, or prescription drug management. Billing 99213 for simple insulin refill without clinical evaluation documentation triggers downcoding to 99212. Chronic care management (99490) can be billed separately for qualifying patients.

cpt_description: Total Hip Arthroplasty
billing_rule: Total hip replacement requires prior authorization and documented conservative treatment failure: physical therapy (minimum 6 weeks), NSAIDs or analgesics, and possible corticosteroid injections. Radiographic evidence of advanced OA (bone-on-bone) must be documented. Patient BMI >40 may trigger additional medical clearance requirements. Cannot bill bilateral hip replacement on same day as unilateral without modifier 50.

cpt_description: Psychiatric Diagnostic Evaluation
billing_rule: Initial psychiatric evaluation (90791) covered once per new episode or new provider. Cannot bill 90791 multiple times for same patient within 12 months without documentation of new onset, significant clinical change, or new provider. Follow-up visits use 90832-90838 series. Billing 90791 and 99213 on same day for same provider is not allowed. Documentation must include DSM-5 diagnostic criteria met.
frequency_limit: Once per new episode; not annually for stable patients

cpt_description: Left Heart Catheterization with Coronary Angiography
billing_rule: Coronary angiography is covered for acute MI, unstable angina, or high-risk stress test. In STEMI, emergent cath is covered without prior auth. In NSTEMI, covered within 72 hours. Elective cath post-MI requires documentation of residual ischemia. Cannot bill left and right heart catheterization (93460) when only left-sided cath performed. Hospital must be CATH-approved facility.
frequency_limit: Per acute episode or documented ischemia

cpt_description: Ambulatory Continuous Glucose Monitor (CGM) placement and training
billing_rule: CGM devices covered under Medicare for Type 2 diabetics who are on intensive insulin therapy (3+ injections/day or insulin pump). Not covered for diet-controlled or non-insulin-dependent Type 2 DM under Medicare. Most commercial payers cover CGM for any insulin-using diabetic. Documentation must confirm insulin regimen and provider order. Cannot bill CGM training (95249) and placement (95250) on same day without separate documentation.
frequency_limit: Sensor replacement every 7-14 days depending on device

cpt_description: Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
billing_rule: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy for symptomatic gallstones (biliary colic confirmed by ultrasound) is covered. Asymptomatic gallstones in the absence of complications are generally NOT covered for elective surgery. Documentation must confirm symptomatic episodes with ultrasound-confirmed gallstones. Prior auth required by most commercial payers. Open cholecystectomy (47600) billed separately if conversion from laparoscopic required.

cpt_description: Pulmonary Stress Testing (simple)
billing_rule: Exercise pulmonary stress testing for asthma evaluation covered when exercise-induced bronchospasm is suspected. Spirometry pre- and post-bronchodilator (94010, 94060) is first-line before stress testing. Stress testing during active exacerbation is contraindicated and will be denied. Documentation must include current medication regimen and symptom severity score (ACQ or ACT).
frequency_limit: Once for initial evaluation; repeat with clinical change

icd_description: Derangement of unspecified lateral meniscus due to old tear
cpt_description: Arthroscopy, knee, surgical with meniscectomy
billing_rule: Arthroscopic meniscectomy requires prior auth and documented conservative treatment failure (physical therapy for 6+ weeks, NSAIDs, activity modification). MRI confirming meniscal tear required. Degenerative meniscal tears in patients with OA may not be covered — evidence shows surgical outcomes equivalent to physical therapy for degenerative tears. Age and OA grade are important factors in coverage decisions.
frequency_limit: Once per knee per tear episode

cpt_description: Infusion therapy for MS disease-modifying treatment
billing_rule: DMT infusion (natalizumab, ocrelizumab, alemtuzumab) requires prior auth with documentation of relapsing-remitting MS confirmed by MRI (McDonald criteria), EDSS score, and failure or intolerance of first-line oral DMTs. Step therapy protocols vary by payer. REMS program enrollment required for natalizumab. Infusion site must be accredited. Annual re-authorization required with documented clinical response.
frequency_limit: Per approved dosing schedule (monthly/quarterly/annually depending on drug)

cpt_description: Influenza vaccine, quadrivalent, preservative free
billing_rule: Annual influenza vaccination covered 100% under ACA for all ages without copay when billed as preventive. Must use vaccine administration code (90471) separately from vaccine supply code. Billing flu vaccine with a diagnostic ICD (instead of Z23) may result in cost-sharing applied to patient. High-dose vaccine (90688) covered for patients 65+ years. Cannot bill same influenza vaccine twice in one season.

cpt_description: CPAP device
billing_rule: CPAP device covered under Medicare DME benefit for OSA confirmed by sleep study (AHI ≥5 with symptoms OR AHI ≥15 without symptoms). Requires 90-day compliance trial: patient must use CPAP 4+ hours/night on 70%+ of nights in a consecutive 30-day period within first 90 days. Non-compliance leads to denial of ongoing supply coverage. Documentation of follow-up visit confirming compliance is required for continued coverage.

icd_description: Morbid (severe) obesity due to excess calories
cpt_description: Laparoscopic Roux-en-Y gastric bypass with EEA anastomosis
billing_rule: Requires documented prior authorization. Patient BMI must be strictly > 40, or > 35 with severe comorbidities (e.g., uncontrolled hypertension, severe sleep apnea). Clinical documentation MUST include proof of failure of a 6-month medically supervised weight loss program. Nutritional and psychological clearance evaluations must be completed prior to surgery approval.
frequency_limit: Once per lifetime under standard Medicare/commercial plans

icd_description: Malignant neoplasm of unspecified part of unspecified bronchus or lung
cpt_description: Injection, pembrolizumab (Keytruda), 1 mg
billing_rule: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) requires strict prior authorization. Must be billed with the administration code (e.g., 96413). Documentation must confirm PD-L1 expression levels (e.g., TPS >= 50% for first-line NSCLC) or MSI-H/dMMR status. Off-label use is strictly denied unless supported by NCCN guidelines compendium. Precise dosage tracking is audited.
frequency_limit: Per FDA approved dosing schedule (typically every 3 or 6 weeks)

cpt_description: Arthrodesis, posterior interbody technique, single interspace; lumbar
billing_rule: Lumbar spinal fusion requires documented failure of at least 6 months of conservative treatment (PT, NSAIDs, epidural injections). Radiographic evidence of instability or severe stenosis must be present. Must include neurosurgical clearance. Cannot bill 22630 alongside decompression codes for the exact same interspace unless distinct pathology exists.

cpt_description: Injection(s), interlaminar epidural or subarachnoid, lumbar or sacral; with imaging guidance
billing_rule: Epidural Steroid Injections (ESI) require documentation of radicular pain unresponsive to 4 weeks of conservative therapy. Imaging guidance (fluoroscopy or CT) is required and included in the CPT 62323. Must document pain scale (e.g., VAS > 5/10) pre-injection. Maximum of 3-4 injections per rolling 12-month period. Series of 3 injections without functional improvement is grounds for denial of further injections.

cpt_description: Insertion of new or replacement of permanent pacemaker with transvenous electrode(s); atrial and ventricular
billing_rule: Permanent pacemaker insertion requires ECG or Holter monitor documentation confirming complete AV block, symptomatic Mobitz II, or symptomatic bradycardia. Prior auth required by commercial plans (Medicare exempts emergent cases). Generator and leads must be billed with appropriate HCPCS device codes (C-codes) for hospital billing. Cannot bill separate fluoroscopy guidance.

cpt_description: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy, flexible, transoral; with biopsy, single or multiple
billing_rule: EGD with biopsy requires documented failure of PPI trial for at least 8 weeks for uncomplicated GERD. Alarm symptoms (dysphagia, weight loss, bleeding) bypass the PPI trial requirement. Biopsy must be documented in pathology report. Cannot bill 43239 and 43235 (diagnostic EGD) on same day.
frequency_limit: Once per episode; repeat depends on pathology

icd_description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with unspecified diabetic retinopathy with macular edema
cpt_description: Intravitreal injection of a pharmacologic agent
billing_rule: Intravitreal injection (e.g., anti-VEGF therapy) requires concurrent billing of the drug (e.g., J0178 for Eylea). Documentation must include OCT imaging confirming macular edema and visual acuity testing. Prior authorization required for most anti-VEGF agents. Bilateral injections must use modifier 50 and precise drug unit accounting.
frequency_limit: Typically every 4-8 weeks per eye depending on agent

icd_description: Basal cell carcinoma of skin of nose
cpt_description: Mohs micrographic technique; first stage
billing_rule: Mohs surgery is only covered for high-risk areas (e.g., face, nose, lips) or recurrent tumors. Routine excision (11440 series) should be used for trunk/extremities unless specific criteria are met. Surgeon must act as both surgeon and pathologist. Operative report must detail size of defect, number of tissue blocks, and depth. Reconstruction billed separately but subject to NCCI edits.

cpt_description: Cystourethroscopy, with biopsy(s)
billing_rule: Cystoscopy with biopsy for hematuria requires prior urinalysis confirming microscopic or macroscopic hematuria. Documentation must state biopsy was taken due to suspicious lesion found during scope. Cannot bill diagnostic cystoscopy (52000) on same date. Pathology report must accompany claim for audit protection.

cpt_description: MRI, brain; without contrast followed by with contrast
billing_rule: Brain MRI for new-onset seizures is widely covered. Routine follow-up MRI for stable epilepsy requires clinical justification (change in seizure semiology, new focal deficits). Prior authorization required by commercial payers. Cannot bill concurrent CT without separate distinct medical necessity.

icd_description: Encounter for general adult medical examination without abnormal findings
cpt_description: Initial comprehensive preventive medicine evaluation and management
billing_rule: Preventive medicine exams are covered once annually. If an abnormality is encountered and requires significant, separately identifiable evaluation, append modifier 25 to the appropriate E/M code. Do not bill a sick visit for minor, incidental findings.

cpt_description: Emergency department visit, moderate-high complexity
billing_rule: Level 4 ED visits require documentation of a detailed history, detailed examination, and medical decision making of moderate complexity. Ordering advanced imaging (CT/MRI) or prescribing prescription medications typically supports this level.

cpt_description: Immunization administration through 18 years of age
billing_rule: Must be billed with counseling by a physician or qualified healthcare professional. Billed per vaccine component. Requires a corresponding vaccine product code.

cpt_description: Arthroplasty, acetabular and proximal femoral prosthetic replacement (total hip arthroplasty)
billing_rule: Total hip arthroplasty requires prior authorization. Documentation must include severe pain limiting ADLs, failure of at least 3 months of conservative therapy (NSAIDs, PT, walking aids), and radiographic evidence of severe joint space narrowing or bone-on-bone articulation.
frequency_limit: Once per hip per lifetime (unless revision)

icd_description: Encounter for screening for malignant neoplasm of colon
cpt_description: Colonoscopy, flexible; diagnostic
billing_rule: Screening colonoscopy is covered every 10 years for average-risk individuals. Do not bill for patients under 45 unless high-risk documentation (family history, specific genetic syndromes) is provided. If a polyp is found and removed, use modifier PT (Medicare) or 33 (Commercial) to waive patient cost-sharing.

icd_description: Encounter for supervision of normal first pregnancy
cpt_description: Routine obstetric care including antepartum care, vaginal delivery, and postpartum care
billing_rule: Global maternity care covers all routine prenatal visits, the delivery, and postpartum care. Only bill after delivery. Do not bill separate E/M codes for routine pregnancy visits unless the visit is for a completely unrelated condition (e.g., strep throat).

cpt_description: Cystourethroscopy, with ureteroscopy and/or pyeloscopy; with lithotripsy and insertion of indwelling ureteral stent
billing_rule: Stent insertion is included in 52356 and cannot be billed separately using 52332. Documentation must confirm the size and location of the stone and that lithotripsy was actively performed. Bilateral procedures require modifier 50.

icd_description: Malignant neoplasm of unspecified site of right female breast
cpt_description: Radiation treatment management, 5 treatments
billing_rule: Radiation treatment management is billed once for every 5 fractions (treatments). The physician must have a face-to-face interaction with the patient to evaluate the treatment progress and any side effects at least once during the 5 treatments.

icd_description: Migraine without aura, not intractable, with status migrainosus
cpt_description: Chemodenervation of muscle(s); bilateral
billing_rule: Botox for chronic migraines requires documentation of 15 or more headache days per month, with headaches lasting 4 or more hours, and failure of at least two classes of prophylactic medications (e.g., beta-blockers, anti-convulsants). Billed with corresponding J-code for the Botox supply.

cpt_description: Injection(s), diagnostic or therapeutic agent, paravertebral facet joint with image guidance, cervical or thoracic; single level
billing_rule: Facet joint injections require documented failure of conservative therapy (NSAIDs, PT). Image guidance (fluoroscopy or CT) is required. Maximum of 2 diagnostic blocks. Subsequent therapeutic injections require documentation of at least 50% pain relief from the diagnostic block.

icd_description: Rheumatoid arthritis with rheumatoid factor of unspecified site without organ or systems involvement
cpt_description: Injection, infliximab, 10 mg
billing_rule: Infliximab infusions require severe active disease and documented failure or intolerance to conventional DMARDs (e.g., methotrexate). Billed per 10 mg units. Must be accompanied by the appropriate infusion administration code (e.g., 96413). Ensure precise vial wastage tracking (modifier JW) to avoid audits.

icd_description: Major depressive disorder, single episode, severe without psychotic features
cpt_description: Psychotherapy, 45 minutes with patient
billing_rule: Psychotherapy sessions between 38 and 52 minutes face-to-face. Requires a documented treatment plan with specific goals. Cannot be billed concurrently with 90832 (30 min) or 90837 (60 min) on the same date. If E/M is performed, use add-on code 90836 instead.

cpt_description: Computed tomography, head or brain; without contrast material
billing_rule: CT head for unspecified headache is often denied without red flag symptoms documented (e.g., worst headache of life, focal neurological deficit, new onset in age >50). Routine imaging for tension or migraine headaches without new/changing features is not medically necessary.

cpt_description: Destruction of benign lesions other than skin tags or cutaneous vascular proliferative lesions; up to 14 lesions
billing_rule: Destruction of benign lesions for purely cosmetic reasons is excluded. Medical necessity requires documentation of bleeding, intense itching, pain, or inflammation. Billed based on the number of lesions, not the method of destruction. Do not use for skin tags (use 11200).
frequency_limit: Varies; rarely covered repeatedly for benign lesions

cpt_description: Ophthalmological services: medical examination and evaluation; comprehensive, established patient
billing_rule: Comprehensive eye exams are covered for medical conditions like glaucoma. Routine vision exams for refractive error (glasses) are typically excluded by medical insurance and require vision insurance. Documentation must show a dilated fundus exam unless contraindicated.
frequency_limit: Once per year for stable disease

cpt_description: Thyroid stimulating hormone (TSH)
billing_rule: TSH testing is the primary initial test for thyroid dysfunction. Routine concurrent testing of Free T3 and Free T4 is often denied unless the TSH is abnormal. Frequency of testing for stable patients on levothyroxine replacement is typically every 6 to 12 months.
frequency_limit: Once every 6 months unless medication adjusted

cpt_description: Bronchodilation responsiveness, spirometry as in 94010, pre- and post-bronchodilator administration
billing_rule: Pre- and post-bronchodilator spirometry requires documentation of both tests. It is used to assess reversibility of airway obstruction. Cannot be billed on the same day as 94010 (routine spirometry) or 94070 (bronchospasm provocation).

cpt_description: Therapeutic procedure, 1 or more areas, each 15 minutes; therapeutic exercises
billing_rule: Therapeutic exercises are time-based codes (requires 8-22 minutes for 1 unit). Must document specific exercises performed and how they relate to the functional goals in the Plan of Care. Requires active physician certification of the POC every 90 days for Medicare.
frequency_limit: Subject to annual caps unless KX modifier justified

cpt_description: Nasal interface (mask or cannula type) used with positive airway pressure device
billing_rule: Replacement CPAP masks are covered only if the patient has met the initial 90-day compliance requirements and there is documented ongoing use and benefit. The typical Medicare allowance for a nasal mask replacement is once every 3 months.

cpt_description: Infectious agent detection by nucleic acid (DNA or RNA); HIV-1, quantified
billing_rule: HIV viral load testing is covered to monitor disease progression or response to ART. Frequency should not exceed every 3 to 4 months for stable patients. Testing more frequently requires documentation of a change in clinical status or medication.

cpt_description: Percutaneous tests (scratch, puncture, prick) with allergenic extracts
billing_rule: Allergy testing is covered when conservative management has failed or when identification of specific allergens is required for immunotherapy. Number of tests must be medically reasonable (typically up to 70 tests). Documentation must identify the specific allergens tested.
frequency_limit: Once per 1-3 years depending on clinical history

cpt_description: Reduction mammaplasty
billing_rule: Reduction mammaplasty requires strict prior authorization. Documentation must prove the procedure is for medical reasons (macromastia causing severe back/neck pain, intertrigo) and not cosmetic. Requires documentation of conservative therapy failure (e.g., PT, supportive garments) and specific estimated resection weight per breast based on the Schnur Sliding Scale.

icd_description: Varicose veins of right lower extremity with pain
cpt_description: Endovenous ablation therapy of incompetent vein, extremity, inclusive of all imaging guidance and monitoring, percutaneous, radiofrequency; first vein treated
billing_rule: Radiofrequency ablation for varicose veins requires documented symptomatic disease (pain, swelling, ulceration) and failure of a 3-6 month trial of conservative therapy (graduated compression stockings). Duplex ultrasound must document reflux > 500 ms in the treated vein segment.

icd_description: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with other specified complication
cpt_description: Debridement of nail(s) by any method(s); 1 to 5
billing_rule: Routine foot care is generally excluded from Medicare coverage unless the patient has a systemic condition (like diabetes with peripheral neuropathy) that makes the procedure hazardous if performed by a nonprofessional. Must document specific class findings (e.g., absent pulses, neuropathy). Append modifier Q7, Q8, or Q9 as appropriate.

cpt_description: Arthroplasty, knee, condyle and plateau; medical and lateral compartments with or without patella resurfacing (total knee arthroplasty)
billing_rule: Total Knee Arthroplasty (TKA) requires documented failure of at least 3 months of conservative treatment (e.g., NSAIDs, physical therapy, intra-articular injections) unless contraindicated. Radiographic evidence of severe joint space narrowing, subchondral sclerosis, or osteophytes must be present. Routine BMI and smoking cessation clearance are increasingly required by commercial payers.
frequency_limit: Once per knee per lifetime (excluding revisions)

icd_description: Complete rotator cuff tear or rupture of right shoulder, not specified as traumatic
cpt_description: Arthroscopy, shoulder, surgical; with rotator cuff repair
billing_rule: Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair requires MRI documentation of a full-thickness tear or a partial tear failing 6 weeks of conservative therapy (PT, injections). Cannot bill diagnostic arthroscopy (29805) concurrently with surgical arthroscopy in the same shoulder. Debridement (29822 or 29823) is bundled unless performed in a separate anatomical region of the shoulder.

cpt_description: Percutaneous transcatheter placement of intracoronary stent(s), with coronary angioplasty when performed; single major coronary artery or branch
billing_rule: PCI/Stent placement in the setting of NSTEMI or STEMI does not require prior authorization (emergent/urgent). For elective PCI, strictly requires prior auth, evidence of ischemia on non-invasive testing, and >70% stenosis. Cannot bill diagnostic catheterization separately on the same day if the decision to intervene was made prior to the procedure.
frequency_limit: Once per coronary artery per encounter

cpt_description: Comprehensive electrophysiologic evaluation with intracardiac catheter ablation of atrial fibrillation by pulmonary vein isolation
billing_rule: AFib ablation (PVI) requires documented failure, intolerance, or contraindication to at least one Class I or III antiarrhythmic drug. Requires TEE (93312) prior to or during the procedure to rule out left atrial appendage thrombus. Mapping (93613) and left atrial pacing are bundled and cannot be billed separately.

cpt_description: Colonoscopy, flexible; with removal of tumor(s), polyp(s), or other lesion(s) by snare technique
billing_rule: When a screening colonoscopy turns diagnostic due to polyp removal, append modifier PT (Medicare) or 33 (Commercial) to waive patient cost-sharing. If multiple polyps are removed via snare in different sections, bill 45385 only once. If a separate polyp is removed via biopsy forceps (45380), bill 45385 and 45380-59.

icd_description: Calculus of bile duct without cholangitis or cholecystitis
cpt_description: Endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP); with removal of calculi/debris from biliary/pancreatic duct(s)
billing_rule: ERCP for stone removal requires ultrasound, CT, or MRCP documentation showing biliary dilation or filling defects, plus elevated LFTs/bilirubin. Diagnostic ERCP (43260) is bundled and cannot be billed with therapeutic ERCP (43264). Sphincterotomy (43262) is included in 43264 if performed to remove the stone.

icd_description: Malignant neoplasm of unspecified site of left female breast
cpt_description: Chemotherapy administration, intravenous infusion technique; up to 1 hour, single or initial substance/drug
billing_rule: Initial chemotherapy administration code. Can only be billed once per encounter. Additional hours of the same drug must use add-on code 96415. Additional different drugs use 96417. Hydration (96360) is bundled if run concurrently, and can only be billed if given sequentially and medically necessary for toxicity management.

icd_description: Malignant neoplasm of unspecified part of unspecified bronchus or lung
cpt_description: Positron emission tomography (PET) with concurrently acquired computed tomography (CT) for attenuation correction and anatomical localization imaging
billing_rule: PET/CT for initial staging of NSCLC is broadly covered. However, PET/CT for routine surveillance in asymptomatic patients after curative treatment is strictly non-covered by Medicare. Post-treatment PET requires suspicion of recurrence (e.g., rising tumor markers, new symptoms, equivocal CT findings). Append modifier PI for initial staging and PS for subsequent staging.
frequency_limit: Max 3 post-treatment scans per lifetime without strict appeal

cpt_description: Extracapsular cataract removal with insertion of intraocular lens prosthesis (1 stage procedure)
billing_rule: Cataract surgery requires documentation of visual acuity of 20/40 or worse, and a statement that the cataract impairs the patient's Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) such as reading or driving at night. A-scan biometry (76519) for IOL measurement is bundled into the global package if performed on the same day.

icd_description: Malignant melanoma of other part of trunk
cpt_description: Excision, malignant lesion including margins, trunk, arms, or legs; excised diameter 2.1 to 3.0 cm
billing_rule: Excision of malignant lesions must include the pathology report proving malignancy (or suspicious findings justifying excision). The excised diameter is measured as the lesion size plus the narrowest margins required. Simple repair (12001-12007) is bundled. Intermediate or complex repair can be billed separately with modifier 59/51.

cpt_description: Psychiatric diagnostic evaluation
billing_rule: Initial psychiatric diagnostic evaluation without medical services. Covered once per episode of illness. If medical services (e.g., prescribing medications, physical exam) are performed, use 90792. Cannot be billed on the same day as E/M services or psychotherapy by the same provider.

icd_description: Epilepsy, unspecified, not intractable, without status epilepticus
cpt_description: Electroencephalogram (EEG); including recording awake and drowsy
billing_rule: Routine EEG is covered for diagnosis of seizures, syncope, or altered mental status. Extended EEG (video or >24 hours) requires justification such as characterizing spell types or pre-surgical evaluation for refractory epilepsy. Must document patient state (awake/asleep) and activation procedures (hyperventilation, photic stimulation).

icd_description: Benign prostatic hyperplasia with lower urinary tract symptoms
cpt_description: Transurethral electrosurgical resection of prostate (TURP)
billing_rule: TURP requires documentation of moderate-to-severe LUTS (IPSS >7) and failure of alpha-blocker/5-ARI medical therapy, or indications such as refractory urinary retention, recurrent UTIs, or gross hematuria. Cystoscopy is strictly bundled. Includes a 90-day global period.

cpt_description: Pressurized or nonpressurized inhalation treatment for acute airway obstruction
billing_rule: Nebulizer treatment in the clinic for acute exacerbation. Can be billed multiple times per day if multiple distinct treatments are provided, using modifier 76 for repeat procedures. Must be billed with the HCPCS J-code for the medication (e.g., Albuterol J7613).
frequency_limit: Multiple per day allowed with modifier 76

cpt_description: Ambulatory continuous glucose monitoring of interstitial tissue fluid
billing_rule: Professional CGM placement and training. Covered for patients with diabetes requiring multiple daily insulin injections (MDI) or insulin pump therapy, or those with documented recurrent hypoglycemia/hypoglycemic unawareness. Cannot be billed more frequently than once a month.
